title: SOC 2 Report Evaluation Agent
problem: Compliance teams manually review SOC 2 reports against FFIEC examination frameworks, rating User Entity Controls (UECs) and User Relevant Specified Criteria — a multi-day process prone to inconsistency.
solution: Agent ingests a SOC 2 report, evaluates each control against the FFIEC framework (producing CUEC/URSIT ratings), generates a structured evaluation document, uploads to SharePoint, and notifies the team in Slack.
whoItsFor: Compliance Officers, Internal Audit Teams, Third-Party Risk Management
workflowSteps: SOC 2 PDF uploaded by compliance analyst
Agent extracts control descriptions section by section
For each control: evaluates against FFIEC CUEC and URSIT criteria, assigns rating and confidence
Generates structured evaluation document with ratings, gaps, and remediation notes
Uploads completed evaluation to SharePoint in the correct audit folder
Posts summary notification to Slack compliance channel
keyInsights: The evaluation framework as a system prompt (not fine-tuning) is the key pattern. The agent needs to produce consistent, auditable ratings — the evaluation rubric must be versioned alongside the prompt, just like code.

relatedExams.why: Governance, Safety & Risk Management — compliance automation
Eval, Testing, and Debugging — LLM-as-evaluator pattern

mermaidDiagram:
flowchart TD
  A([SOC 2 PDF Upload]) --> B[Extract Controls Section by Section]
  B --> C[Evaluate vs FFIEC Framework]
  C --> D{Rating per Control}
  D -->|Pass| E[CUEC or URSIT Rating]
  D -->|Gap Found| F[Flag + Remediation Note]
  E & F --> G[Structured Evaluation Report]
  G --> H([SharePoint Upload])
  G --> I([Slack Notification])
architectureNotes: The agent ingests a SOC 2 report PDF and an internal control catalog. For each control in the catalog it runs a targeted extraction pass to find the matching section in the report, then applies an evaluator model to score coverage (Met / Partial / Gap). A summary table is emitted alongside a risk narrative and recommended remediation items ordered by impact.
